site.name: Anatomia 3D
site.description: Bilingual interactive platform for learning human anatomy in three dimensions.

home.heroTitle:
The human body,
in three dimensions.
home.heroSubtitle: Rotate, explore, and virtually dissect every organ, bone, and muscle. Built for medical students, teachers, and the curious.
home.featureSystemsBody: Skeletal, muscular, nervous, cardiovascular, respiratory and more — explore each system alone or combined.
home.featureInteractiveBody: Click any structure to reveal its name, description, and function, in French or English.
home.featureLayerBody: A single slider takes you from skin to muscle to skeleton — like a virtual dissection.
home.featureMobileBody: Smooth touch controls on phone, precise mouse controls on desktop. Nothing to install.
home.footer: © {year} Anatomia 3D — Anatomical content under GPL v3.

atlas.tapHint: Tap a structure to learn more
atlas.clickHint: Click a structure to learn more
atlas.noSelectionBody: Click or tap any organ in the 3D scene to see its detailed information here.

about.title: About Anatomia 3D
about.intro: Anatomia 3D is a free and open educational tool to explore human anatomy in three dimensions, in both French and English.
about.modelsBody: The current version ships with a simplified illustrative anatomy model. The project is designed to drop in the detailed Z-Anatomy models (GPL v3) as soon as they are placed in the public/models/ folder.
about.techBody: Next.js 15, React, TypeScript, Three.js (via React Three Fiber), Tailwind CSS, next-intl. Open-source under GPL v3.
about.creditsBody: Detailed anatomical models come from the Z-Anatomy project — a free anatomical atlas under GPL v3. Thanks to their contributors.
about.disclaimerBody: Anatomia 3D is an educational tool. It is not a substitute for medical advice or a clinical anatomy textbook.
